segment_analysis.reason: Visualize the technical mismatch in rail gauges between European and Soviet networks to highlight a physical logistical constraint.

segment_meta.text: This means every single train moving stolen grain, extracted oil, or military reinforcements has to be halted at the old borders, manually unloaded, and
segment_meta.previous_text: physical limitations of infrastructure. The entire Soviet rail network uses a wider track gauge than the standard European lines.
segment_meta.next_text: transferred to different railcars. This creates a perpetual, massive supply chain bottleneck that engineers cannot simply wave away.

segment_plan.reason: Bridges the technical gauge graphic to the physical reality of halted trains and manual unloading operations.

selected_cluster_plan.topic_idea: What if germany would have won world war 2?

selected_cluster.fit_reason: WWII is the most famous historical hinge; exploring an Axis victory requires a strict causal chain, logistics-based plausibility, and avoiding lazy mythmaking, matching this cluster's core demands.

selected_cluster_plan.narrative_mode_reason: A process explainer perfectly structures the 'chained structure' and 'step-by-step build' needed to show exactly how a German victory would unfold through logistics, resources, and timing.

worldview: timing and logistics dictate outcomes over hero worship
single divergence works only through chained structure
broad consequences of a WWII Axis victory must stay causally grounded
pain_growth: attrition and resource strain explain outcomes
losses and endurance are meaningful only when causally linked
hardship is accepted as system cost rather than melodrama
fears_desires: desire for coherent chain reaction from a famous hinge
desire for plausible explanation over spectacle
usage_notes: Start with one clear divergence point for the German victory and immediately state the scenario limits.
Tie every new level of escalation or post-war consequence to resources, timing, or institutional incentives.
Use humor only if it does not replace the causal chain of events.
Ground the largest consequences in the finale again to maintain trust and prevent scope drift.
avoid_notes: Do not rely on pure spectacle-worldbuilding without structural support.
Avoid deus ex machina events in the final act to explain long-term stability or collapse.
Do not broaden the scope beyond what the title promises.
Avoid late speculative overreach and weak causal bridging.

visual_narration_strategy.visual_goal: Показать логистические, пространственные и ресурсные барьеры альтернативного сценария через наглядные причинно-следственные связи, исключая голливудскую зрелищность.
preferred_capture_styles: фокус на индустриальные и логистические объекты
continuity_rules: каждый новый визуальный тезис логически вытекает из предыдущего графического состояния
строгая геопространственная привязка при смене локаций
единая палитра для графики и карт
transition_rules: плавные движения по карте вместо резкой смены декораций
прямые склейки при сопоставлении цифр и фактов
использование линий снабжения как направляющих для перехода
repetition_limits: повторное использование глобальной карты допускается только для фиксации новых ограничивающих факторов
избегать зацикливания одинаковой хроники боевых действий
tone_rules: визуальная сухость в противовес драматическому пафосу
usage_notes: использовать карты для демонстрации пространственных и инфраструктурных тупиков
визуализировать истощение ресурсов через графики на фоне реальных объектов индустрии
avoid_notes: категорически избегать фантастических образов высокотехнологичного дистопичного будущего
не использовать героические или эмоционально манипулятивные кадры
отказаться от визуального перегруза и лишнего графического шума

shot_planning.reason: This segment focuses on the specific logistical process of transferring supplies between different rail gauges, making it a procedural interaction that requires clarity on the mechanical workflow.

videoClipPrompts: Medium-scale side view of the railway border station showing the abrupt end of wide Soviet-gauge tracks beside narrower European standard-gauge tracks with track junction infrastructure in foreground, stationary freight trains loaded with sacks and barrels on both gauges in midground, and industrial warehouses and cargo cranes aligned along rails in background; slow horizontal pan following tracks from wider Soviet gauge on left to narrower European gauge on right with subtle vertical tilt revealing multiple layers of freight cars and workers manually unloading sacks and barrels from Soviet-gauge wagons and transferring them to European-gauge wagons, occasional small movements of crates sliding or rolling and workers gesturing coordination; foreground rails and wagons mostly static while midground workers show steady manual movements and background cranes slowly oscillate; anchored elements include stark difference of track gauges, stationary freight trains loaded with grain sacks and oil barrels, and industrial structures with spatial consistency maintaining positions of tracks, freight cars, workers, warehouses, and cranes; clear silhouettes and distinct layered separation enable readable loading and unloading actions.

Medium-scale side view of the railway border station focusing on the transshipment platform positioned between Soviet broad gauge tracks in the foreground and narrower European gauge tracks in the midground, with stationary freight trains loaded with grain sacks and oil barrels, industrial warehouse buildings and cargo cranes aligned along the rails in the background. Subtle horizontal tracking motion gently following the workflow from left (Soviet-gauge wagons) to right (European-gauge wagons), combined with a slow vertical reveal emphasizing workers’ manual unloading and transfer activities on the platform. Workers manually unloading sacks and barrels from Soviet-gauge freight cars and methodically transferring them onto European-gauge wagons, with occasional sliding or rolling movement of crates and barrels on the platform, and slow oscillation of cargo cranes in the background. Foreground and stationary trains remain mostly static; midground workers show continuous deliberate human-scale manual movements; background cranes exhibit slow, gentle oscillations to provide depth and parallax effect. Distinct contrast between broad Soviet gauge tracks and narrower European tracks; stationary freight trains loaded with grain sacks and oil barrels; industrial buildings and aligned cargo cranes in background. Maintain consistent spatial arrangement of railway tracks, wagons, loading platform, workers, and background industrial structures to preserve the logical transshipment environment within a flat 2D illustrated world. Clear silhouettes of workers engaged in manual transfer actions; distinct separation of foreground rails and wagons from midground workers and background industrial elements; easily decipherable workflow of unloading and reloading cargo without visual clutter.
